title: ee.mesh.hello.error.v1
description: Hello-handshake decline response. PRIVACY INVARIANT: this payload MUST NOT carry responder-side metadata (`responderEeVersion`, `responderWorkspaceIds`, `responderCapabilities`, `responderAdvertisedTags`) — a 'no' from the responder must not leak who we are. The only fields are the schema marker, the echoed requestId, the decline code, an optional human-readable detail, and a discoveryConsent=false marker for caller-side branching symmetry.

discoveryConsent.description: Always false in an error response.

code.description: Stable decline-reason vocabulary. The caller maps each code to its own skip-reason classification in SRR6.46.2 (`incompatible_protocol`, `no_discovery_consent`, `probe_timeout` for `responder_busy`, etc.).

detail.description: Optional human-readable detail. MUST NOT contain responder identity material (node key, IP, tailnet id) — those would leak metadata the privacy invariant is designed to suppress. Suitable for short responder-side hints like 'rate limit window 60s' or 'minimum protocol version 1.0'.
